title: GoNext Plugin Manifest (gonext.io/v1)
description: JSON Schema for the manifest.json shipped inside every .gnplugin bundle. The Install path validates against this schema before any storage write or runtime load. See docs/02-plugin-system.md.

apiVersion.description: Manifest API version. Only one literal is recognised today; future schema revisions will land as a sibling schema with a new literal.

name.description: Platform-unique plugin slug. Lowercase ASCII start, lowercase ASCII + digits + hyphens after, 3..41 chars. Mirrors lifecycle.slugRegex so storage and validator agree.

version.description: Plugin version. Strict Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 — MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional pre-release and build metadata.

entry.description: Path inside the bundle to the WASM module the runtime will instantiate. POSIX-style, no leading slash, no parent traversal.

capabilities.description: Capabilities the plugin requests from the host. Each entry is a dotted token drawn from the platform capability catalog (e.g. "kv.read", "audit.emit"). Operators see this list at install time before activating.

hooks.description: Actions and filters the plugin subscribes to. Action hooks are fire-and-forget; filter hooks transform a value through the pipeline.

jobs.description: Background TaskSpec ids the plugin owns. Validated as a flat list of dotted ids; the scheduler resolves them against its registry at activation time.

requires.description: Compatibility requirements expressed as semver ranges. Today only the host range is recognised; future fields (abi, runtime) will land here.

host.description: Semver range the platform must satisfy. Accepts the npm-style operators (^, ~, >=, etc.) used by the resolver.

depends.description: Other plugins this plugin requires at activation time. Each entry names a plugin slug and a semver range its version must satisfy. The lifecycle Activate gate refuses to flip the row to Active unless every dependency is installed, currently in the Active state, and reports a version inside the requested range. Install is not gated — operators can install a plugin whose dependencies aren't ready yet, then activate it later.

name.description: Plugin slug of the dependency. Same regex as the top-level name.

version.description: Semver range the dependency's installed version must satisfy. Accepts the same operator vocabulary as requires.host (^, ~, >=, etc.).

signature.description: Detached ed25519 signature over the canonical bundle bytes, lowercase hex. Optional in v1 because the registry handles signing centrally; unsigned bundles are rejected by the operator-side install gate.

storage.description: Optional declarative budgets for the plugin's persistent-storage surface. Only the kv namespace is described today; future fields (db, blob) will land here.

kv.description: Per-plugin Redis-backed KV namespace budget. The host soft-evicts the oldest keys when a write would exceed either ceiling; plugins MUST treat eviction as expected behavior.

max_bytes.description: Cumulative value bytes the plugin may store. Omit for unlimited (operator-overridable; the install gate does not grant unlimited by default).

max_keys.description: Maximum number of keys the plugin may hold. Omit for unlimited.

flags.description: Opt-in capability flags. Each flag switches the plugin into a non-default dispatch mode. Unknown flags are rejected so a typo doesn't silently flip the plugin into legacy behaviour.

apply_filters_batch.description: When true, the host invokes the plugin's filter handlers with a whole []any slice in one call (issue #263). The plugin SDK exposes the batched signature; the per-item ApplyFilters path remains the default for legacy plugins.
